replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the F20/F21 M140i B58 generation — a single-generation engine. All pricing is indicative; actual costs vary by mileage, condition and workshop location.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 1 Series M140i engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent BMW specialist) or £120–£170/hr (London/SE dealer). The B58's inline-six layout is relatively accessible compared to V-configuration engines.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 1 Series M140i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Worth it on a vehicle worth £15,000–£30,000
Replacement often £5,500–£9,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 1 Series M140i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with documented oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles)
* Cooling system history — water pump and expansion tank checked/replaced
* No whistling from the engine (PCV valve) and no coolant loss
* Post-2015 (B58 design is consistent across all M140i years)
avoidIf: * Any evidence of coolant loss or overheating (cooling system risk)
* Whistling noise from the engine (PCV valve failure)
* Poor service history — the B58 is robust but needs proper oil
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive problems

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The M140i is one of the best modern BMWs — the last rear-wheel-drive hot hatch with a glorious 3.0-litre inline-six. The B58 is a modern classic: robust, tunable and characterful. Cooling system maintenance is the key to longevity — a £400–£1,000 water pump replacement is small change on a car worth £15,000–£30,000. Buy on history, maintain the cooling system, and the M140i will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare BMW 1 Series M140i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 1 Series M140i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle; the B58 should start with a smooth, deep note
* Service History — 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes are essential for the B58
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss — water pump and expansion tank are key risks
* PCV Valve — Any whistling from the engine? Increased oil consumption?
* VANOS Operation — Check low-end torque and smooth power delivery
* Turbo Response — Test acceleration; any hesitation or lag?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a BMW 1 Series M140i engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned BMW 1 Series M140i engine (B58B30) typically costs £5,500–£9,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,300–£1,800. These are specialist-only replacements — the B58 requires BMW specialist expertise. A water pump replacement costs £400–£1,000.

items.question: Is the BMW 1 Series M140i reliable?
items.answer: Yes — the B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most robust modern engines. It's a significant step forward from the N55. The closed-deck design and front-mounted timing chain eliminated the N55's major weaknesses. Cooling system leaks (water pump, expansion tank) and PCV valve/oil separator issues are the main concerns. Regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles) are essential.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW 1 Series M140i?
items.answer: Cooling system leaks — water pump and expansion tank failure (£400–£1,000), PCV valve/oil separator failure (whistling noise, oil consumption, £400–£800), and VANOS solenoid wear (reduced power, hesitation, £300–£600). The B58 is otherwise exceptionally robust.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW 1 Series M140i engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: 1 Series M140i values range from £12,000–£30,000 depending on condition and mileage. A £5,500–£9,500 reconditioned engine represents 20–75% of vehicle value — our 70% BMW scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, low-mileage example, replacement is often justified. For a higher-mileage example with other issues, assess scrap value first.

items.question: How do I find my BMW 1 Series M140i engine code?
items.answer: The M140i uses a single engine code: B58B30. This is BMW's 3.0-litre turbocharged inline-six, introduced in 2015. It replaced the N55 in the M135i. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

items.question: Can I repair the cooling system leak, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — water pump and expansion tank replacement (£400–£1,000) is a straightforward repair on the B58. The water pump is electrically driven and can fail without warning. Replacing both the water pump and expansion tank together is recommended — they degrade at similar rates. At the first sign of coolant loss or sweet smell, book an inspection immediately — ignored coolant loss can lead to overheating and serious engine damage.
